fix: persist last_error for trackers that timeout on Modal (#290)

When a tracker_process_repo Modal container times out (600s), the
container is killed before process_tracker's exception handler can
write last_error. The orchestrator caught the FunctionTimeoutError
but never persisted it to the database, making the failure invisible.

Now _dispatch_changed_trackers tracks which tracker IDs returned a
result dict, and after fn.map completes, updates last_error for any
orphaned trackers whose containers died silently.

Closes #289

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): fix 401 cascade on hard refresh causing premature logout

Root cause: three bugs combining into a self-reinforcing cascade:

1. BACKEND — get_current_user device_id query missing ORDER BY (auth.py)
   After every RT rotation both the old (revoked) and new (unrevoked) RT share
   the same device_id. Without ORDER BY created_at DESC, SQLite returns the
   oldest row first — which is always the revoked one — and raises 401 even
   with a perfectly valid access token.

2. FRONTEND — apiFetch re-reads auth.accessToken after await (client.ts)
   A request that starts with no token (auth.accessToken=null) gets 401 from
   the backend. By the time the response arrives, auth.refresh() may have
   completed and set a token. The live re-read sees a non-null token and
   incorrectly triggers a second auth.refresh() call, starting the cascade.
   Fix: snapshot tokenAtRequestTime before the fetch and use that for the guard.

3. FRONTEND — auth.refresh() clears token on 429 rate-limit (auth.svelte.ts)
   The refresh endpoint rate-limit (60/min) returns 429. The blanket
   `if (!res.ok) clearToken()` treated 429 the same as 401, logging the user
   out when the refresh was merely throttled. 429 now returns null without
   clearing the in-memory token.

On Ctrl+Shift+R the cascade was:
  hard refresh → auth.refresh() rotates RT → old RT revoked →
  get_current_user finds revoked RT → 401 on every API call →
  each 401 triggers another auth.refresh() → more rotations → more revoked RTs →
  eventually 429 rate-limit hit → clearToken() → workbench switches to AuthGate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: join/memberJoined 並行競合によるメンション失敗の根本修正

原因:
- join と memberJoined は別 HTTP リクエストで並行処理される
- memberJoined が parents.A をセット後、join が session.parents を
  {A:null,B:null} で上書きするため parents.A が消える
- かつ memberJoined が pendingStart を確認した時点でまだ false

修正:
1. join.js: session.parents をすでに存在する場合は上書きしない
   (memberJoined が先にセット済みなら保持)
2. server.js: handleJoin 完了後に parents.A がセット済みかチェックし
   pendingStart=true なら即座にシナリオ開始タイマーを起動

これにより join/memberJoined どちらが先でもメンション付き送信が機能する

commit.message:
fix: preRun 通过闭包捕获 moduleOptions 获取 FS

Emscripten 调用 preRun 数组中的函数时不传参数、不绑定 this，
导致 makePreRunForFiles 里 Module 参数和 this 均为 undefined，FS 无法获取。

修复：makePreRunForFiles 接收 moduleOptions 引用，闭包内直接读 moduleRef.FS，
Emscripten 初始化完成后会将 FS 挂载在同一对象上，preRun 时即可访问。

Fix session ID forwarding for Streamable HTTP MCP servers

The init handshake returns a mcp-session-id header that must be included
in subsequent requests. Without it, remote servers can't correlate the
tools/call, resources/read, or prompts/get request with the initialized
session, causing arguments to be lost.

Bump to 0.1.3.
